figures.caption: Fig. 3: Out-of-distribution (OOD) evaluation across understanding and perception. A, Robustness to photometric and geometric perturbations. B, Generalization under semantic interventions. C, Aggregate OOD statistics. D, Encoder attention patterns during fine-grained manipulation.

Fig. 4: Perception as a precision and robustness bottleneck. A, A multi-scale encoder intervention concentrates visual attention and improves stage-wise performance on peg-in-hole. B, Under viewpoint perturbation, encoder-level adaptation matches the gains of full-backbone adaptation.

Fig. 6: MetaFine overview. Top: scalable atomic fine-grained skills. Middle: compositional task graphs probing understanding, perception, and control. Bottom: hybrid real–sim evaluation pipeline, where real scenes are transferred to simulation via mobilephone 3DGS reconstruction and used for large-scale simulation with paired real rollouts for calibrated evaluation.

Fig. 1: MetaFine: a meta-evaluation framework for fine-grained manipulation. A, Letter-block assembly illustrates how understanding, perception, and behavior must jointly succeed for fine-grained manipulation. B, MetaFine provides a three-dimensional diagnostic protocol, a compositional task graph with an asset library, and a hybrid framework for real-world evaluation.

Table D11: PPI-calibrated estimates across three independent paired sets (n = 20 each, N = 1000 unpaired simulation rollouts per policy). “Hardware-only” is the perset real-only estimate. “PPI estimate” combines the paired real–sim outcomes with the unpaired simulation mean via Eq. D5. The gold-standard real-world reference A is computed from 300 independent real rollouts.

Fig. 5: Stage-wise behavior and hybrid real-world validation. A, Stage-wise analysis reveals spatial drift and repetitive behavior.B, Paired real–sim rollouts enable PPI-calibrated physical evaluation.

Fig. 2:MetaFine disentangles manipulation competency. A, Atomic task analysis reveals policy differences in success, robustness, and trajectory smoothness. B, Performance degrades under perturbations. C, Mean trends show that clean performance overestimates generalization.
